name: History of the North
author: Unknown
description:
A seven-volume chronicle tracing the North from the earliest human settlements through the rise and fall of kingdoms now remembered only in ruin and song.

Later scholars treat it as foundational though incomplete — whole centuries along the Spine of the World are summarised in a single page, and several battles are attributed to heroes whose names no other source records.

name: History of Waterdeep
author: Unknown
description:
Six volumes of municipal history — from Ahghairon's founding compact through guild wars, noble lines, and the secret architectures of power beneath Mount Waterdeep.

No single author is credited; Candlekeep's copy shows three different scriptorium hands and a lacuna where a chapter on the Undermountain was razored out.

name: History of Daggerford
author: Unknown
description:
History of Daggerford — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Hilather's Workbook
author: Halaster Blackcloak
description:
Hilather's Workbook — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Halaster Blackcloak is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to circa 132 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Harpering by Moonlight: Approaches to Life
author: Elminster of Shadowdale
description:
Harpering by Moonlight: Approaches to Life — Harper-related lore — coded, partial, and dangerous to quote without looking over your shoulder. Elminster of Shadowdale is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1330s DR.

Non-Harper readers report the prose is readable; Harper readers report it is a test.

name: Haarl's Treatise on Dragonkind
author: Hearl
description:
Haarl's Treatise on Dragonkind — Natural history from hunters, riverfolk, and sages who went outside and wrote down what bit them. Hearl is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1480 DR.

Seasonal addenda in surviving copies suggest the author revised the work every year they lived to see spring.

name: How to Be an Adventurer
author: Unknown
description:
How to Be an Adventurer — An in-universe work listed in Candlekeep indices and frontier libraries throughout the Heartlands. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1312 DR.

Copies vary by scriptorium; Candlekeep treats the title as known though completeness is never guaranteed.

name: Heroes' Feast: A Cookbook
author: Nojen Eptros, Wyk Nenmael, Imrit Welachwe
description:
Heroes' Feast: A Cookbook — Commercial wisdom dressed as prose — prices, routes, guild customs, and when to walk away from a deal. Nojen Eptros, Wyk Nenmael, Imrit Welachwe is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Factors along the Long Road keep folded excerpts in strongboxes next to their scales and seals.

name: History of the Creator Races
author: Unknown
description:
History of the Creator Races — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.
